race.name: Tåssåsen Ski Marathon
race.tagline: One hundred kilometers across Härjedalen's frozen plateau — Sweden's ultimate ultra-distance Nordic test.

climate.description: Härjedalen sits between the coastal lowlands and the Norwegian border mountains. Cold and dry conditions are the norm in March, with reliable snowpack at altitude. Wind exposure on open plateau sections adds challenge.
challenges: Extreme cold and wind on exposed mountain plateau sections
100km distance demands meticulous nutrition and energy management
Remote terrain with long gaps between aid stations
Late winter daylight still limited at this latitude

course.primary: 100km through the mountains and forests of Härjedalen, one of Sweden's most sparsely populated regions. The course includes significant climbing across plateau terrain and descents through birch forest, demanding both endurance and technical skill over an ultra-marathon distance.
course.surface: Machine-groomed classic tracks through mountain and forest terrain
course.grooming: Regional standard grooming — good but stretched over 100km distance
features: 100km ultra-distance — one of the longest Nordic races anywhere
Remote Härjedalen mountain and forest landscape
Open plateau sections with potential wind exposure

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Extreme distance and altitude in remote Härjedalen, but very small field and limited accessibility. A specialist ultra event.

history.summary: The Tåssåsen Ski Marathon is one of Sweden's most extreme ultra-distance ski races, covering 100km through the remote mountains of Härjedalen. Dating from around 1960, it has remained a niche but respected event for skiers seeking distances beyond the standard marathon format. Härjedalen's sparse population and reliable winter conditions make it an authentic wilderness racing experience.
notable_facts: At 100km, one of Sweden's longest ski races
Based in Härjedalen, one of Sweden's least populated regions
Reliable snow conditions due to inland mountain location
Attracts ultra-endurance specialists seeking maximum distance
